pattern: A sliding window of the last k indices, held in a hash set, turns the index-distance condition into a constant-time membership test: a repeat inside the window is exactly a nearby duplicate.
hints: Two equal values matter only when their positions are at most k apart, so a far-apart repeat does not count.
Remember, for each value, the most recent index it appeared at, and check whether the current index minus that one is within k.

Maintain a set of the values in the window of the previous k indices: add the current value, and once the window exceeds k drop the value that fell out the back; a value already in the set means a nearby duplicate.

edge_inputs.description: duplicates exist but always farther than k apart
k=0 can never satisfy distance at least one
k larger than the array still bounded by available indices
all distinct so no duplicate regardless of k

stress.description: values drawn from a small range so collisions are frequent, varied k
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(0, 50) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(0, 20)
    return [nums, k]
stress.note: small value range forces many near and far collisions to test the window boundary
